The '''IRD starfighters''', short for '''Intercept *Reconnaissance *Defense''', was a swift and heavily armed [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] used by the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector's]] [[Picket Fleet/Legends|Picket Fleet]].

==Characteristics==
The IRD featured powerful engines, twin [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], and the best [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] ejection mechanism of any contemporary fighter, but lacked a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]. As a result, the Authority IRD had to be transported by larger vessels such as the [[Marauder-class Assault Corvette/Legends|''Marauder''-class corvette]] or the [[Victory I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]].

==History==
[[File:Ird.jpg|thumb|left|200px|An original IRD (left) and an IRD-A (right)]]
The Corporate Sector Authority designed the Authority IRD fighter for the various missions of the [[Picket Fleet/Legends|Picket Fleet]]. Early Authority IRD models were not aerodynamic and performed so badly in atmospheric combat that many pilots refused to engage targets in [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]; this led to the development of the Authority [[IRD-A starfighter]], which was deployed during atmospheric battles. The IRD-A had a carefully molded fuselage that channeled air through a vector stabilizer, enhancing both maneuverability and speed; it also mounted [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]], making it a tougher opponent than its predecessor.

During [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo's]] days in the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]], Solo and a group of [[Outlaw tech/Legends|outlaw techs]] fought against the new Authority IRD-A fighters, defeating them in early model [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunters]]; this victory was anomalous, however, and other, more successful battles encouraged the Authority to begin a full production run of the IRD-A.

IRD starfighters were also used by [[Falcon's Nest mercenaries|a mercenary group]] during their [[Mission to meet Bothan delegation|attempt to destabilize]] the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] by driving a wedge between the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]] and [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustans]]. IRDs were also seen in use by these mercenaries defending their [[Azzameen Station/Legends|base of operations]] when it [[Battle of Azzameen Station|came under attack]] by the Rebels.

== Behind the Scenes ==
Though labeled in ''Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance'' as the "Authority IRD", the model used in-game is actually that of an IRD-A.
